title: Redsync Sourcey API Documentation
content: This site documents `github.com/go-redsync/redsync/v4`, a maintained BSD-3-Clause Go implementation of the Redis-based distributed mutual exclusion algorithm. The documentation is generated by Sourcey 3.6.3 from the real project source pinned at commit:

title: Pinned Source Coverage
content: This documentation build is bound to the public Redsync source commit:

title: Exact Mapping Checks
content: Pinned Source Coverage - Exact Mapping Checks

title: Hosting Decision
content: Read the Docs is the selected hosting service at `https://redsync-sourcey-docs.readthedocs.io/en/latest/`. Deployment identity is the exact repository commit containing this page. Publication is established only when repository main resolves to that same commit, Read the Docs reports a successful build for that same commit, and anonymous readback matches its generated bytes.

title: Maintainer-Facing Documentation Gap Analysis
content: Redsync exposes a compact root API and several Redis client adapters, but users must discover those surfaces by moving between the README, pkg.go.dev, and individual source directories. The README Documentation section still links to the legacy `godoc.org` URL over HTTP. It does not provide a project-specific, navigable package map or make adapter compatibility easy to compare.

title: Current Gap
content: Maintainer-Facing Documentation Gap Analysis - Current Gap

title: Optional Upstream Proposal Sequence and PR Rationale
content: This page records the maintainer rationale and required phase ordering. The optional link proposal is `https://github.com/go-redsync/redsync/pull/245`. It is currently open and unmerged, so it is not proof that Redsync maintainers adopted this site.

title: github.com/go-redsync/redsync/v4
content: Package redsync provides a Redis-based distributed mutual exclusion lock implementation as described in the post http://redis.io/topics/distlock.

title: var ErrExtendFailed
var ErrFailed
var ErrLockAlreadyExpired
content:
ErrLockAlreadyExpired is the error resulting if trying to unlock the lock which already expired.

var ErrLockAlreadyExpired = errors.New("redsync: failed to unlock, lock was already expired")

title: func New(pools ...redis.Pool) *Redsync
content:
New creates and returns a new Redsync instance from given Redis connection pools.

func New(pools ...redis.Pool) *Redsync

title: func WithDriftFactor(factor float64) Option
content:
WithDriftFactor can be used to set the clock drift factor.
The default value is 0.01.

func WithDriftFactor(factor float64) Option

title: func WithExpiry(expiry time.Duration) Option
content:
WithExpiry can be used to set the expiry of a mutex to the given value.
The default is 8s.

func WithExpiry(expiry time.Duration) Option

title: func WithFailFast(b bool) Option
content:
WithFailFast can be used to quickly acquire and release the lock.
When some Redis servers are blocking, we do not need to wait for responses from all the Redis servers response.
As long as the quorum is met, we can assume the lock is acquired. The effect of this parameter is to achieve low
latency, avoid Redis blocking causing Lock/Unlock to not return for a long time.

func WithFailFast(b bool) Option

title: func WithGenValueFunc(genValueFunc func() (string, error)) Option
content:
WithGenValueFunc can be used to set the custom value generator.

func WithGenValueFunc(genValueFunc func() (string, error)) Option

title: func WithRetryDelay(delay time.Duration) Option
content:
WithRetryDelay can be used to set the amount of time to wait between retries.
The default value is rand(50ms, 250ms).

func WithRetryDelay(delay time.Duration) Option

title: func WithRetryDelayFunc(delayFunc DelayFunc) Option
content:
WithRetryDelayFunc can be used to override default delay behavior.

func WithRetryDelayFunc(delayFunc DelayFunc) Option

title: func WithSetNXOnExtend() Option
content:
WithSetNXOnExtend improves extending logic to extend the key if exist
and if not, tries to set a new key in redis
Useful if your redises restart often and you want to reduce the chances of losing the lock
Read this MR for more info: https://github.com/go-redsync/redsync/pull/149

func WithSetNXOnExtend() Option

title: func WithShufflePools(b bool) Option
content:
WithShufflePools can be used to shuffle Redis pools to reduce centralized access in concurrent scenarios.

func WithShufflePools(b bool) Option

title: func WithTimeoutFactor(factor float64) Option
content:
WithTimeoutFactor can be used to set the timeout factor.
The default value is 0.05.

func WithTimeoutFactor(factor float64) Option

title: func WithTries(tries int) Option
content:
WithTries can be used to set the number of times lock acquire is attempted.
The default value is 32.

func WithTries(tries int) Option

title: func WithValue(v string) Option
content:
WithValue can be used to assign the random value without having to call lock.
This allows the ownership of a lock to be "transferred" and allows the lock to be unlocked from elsewhere.

func WithValue(v string) Option

title: type DelayFunc
content:
A DelayFunc is used to decide the amount of time to wait between retries.

type DelayFunc func(tries int) time.Duration

title: type ErrNodeTaken
type ErrTaken
type Mutex
Mutex.Extend
content:
Extend resets the mutex's expiry and returns the status of expiry extension.

func  Extend() (bool, error)

title: Mutex.ExtendContext
content:
ExtendContext resets the mutex's expiry and returns the status of expiry extension.

func  ExtendContext(ctx context.Context) (bool, error)

title: Mutex.Lock
content:
Lock locks m. In case it returns an error on failure, you may retry to acquire the lock by calling this method again.

func  Lock() error

title: Mutex.LockContext
content:
LockContext locks m. In case it returns an error on failure, you may retry to acquire the lock by calling this method again.

func  LockContext(ctx context.Context) error

title: Mutex.Name
content:
Name returns mutex name (i.e. the Redis key).

func  Name() string

title: Mutex.TryLock
content:
TryLock only attempts to lock m once and returns immediately regardless of success or failure without retrying.

func  TryLock() error

title: Mutex.TryLockContext
content:
TryLockContext only attempts to lock m once and returns immediately regardless of success or failure without retrying.

func  TryLockContext(ctx context.Context) error

title: Mutex.Unlock
content:
Unlock unlocks m and returns the status of unlock.

func  Unlock() (bool, error)

title: Mutex.UnlockContext
content:
UnlockContext unlocks m and returns the status of unlock.

func  UnlockContext(ctx context.Context) (bool, error)

title: Mutex.Until
content:
Until returns the time of validity of acquired lock. The value will be zero value until a lock is acquired.

func  Until() time.Time

title: Mutex.Valid
content:
Valid returns true if the lock acquired through m is still valid. It may
also return true erroneously if quorum is achieved during the call and at
least one node then takes long enough to respond for the lock to expire.

Deprecated: Use Until instead. See https://github.com/go-redsync/redsync/issues/72.

func  Valid() (bool, error)

title: Mutex.ValidContext
content:
ValidContext returns true if the lock acquired through m is still valid. It may
also return true erroneously if quorum is achieved during the call and at
least one node then takes long enough to respond for the lock to expire.

Deprecated: Use Until instead. See https://github.com/go-redsync/redsync/issues/72.

func  ValidContext(ctx context.Context) (bool, error)

title: Mutex.Value
content:
Value returns the current random value. The value will be empty until a lock is acquired (or WithValue option is used).

func  Value() string

title: type Option
type OptionFunc
content:
OptionFunc is a function that configures a mutex.

type OptionFunc func(*Mutex)

title: type RedisError
type Redsync
Redsync.NewMutex
content:
NewMutex returns a new distributed mutex with given name.

func  NewMutex(name string, options ...Option) *Mutex

title: func NewScript(keyCount int, src, hash string) *Script
content:
NewScript returns a new script object. If keyCount is greater than or equal
to zero, then the count is automatically inserted in the EVAL command
argument list. If keyCount is less than zero, then the application supplies
the count as the first value in the keysAndArgs argument to the Do, Send and
SendHash methods.
Taken from https://github.com/gomodule/redigo/blob/46992b0f02f74066bcdfd9b03e33bc03abd10dc7/redis/script.go#L32-L41

func NewScript(keyCount int, src, hash string) *Script

title: type Conn
content:
Conn is a single Redis connection.

type Conn interface {
	Get(name string) (string, error)
	Set(name string, value string) (bool, error)
	SetNX(name string, value string, expiry time.Duration) (bool, error)
	Eval(script *Script, keysAndArgs ...any) (any, error)
	ScriptLoad(script *Script) error
	PTTL(name string) (time.Duration, error)
	Close() error
}

title: type Pool
type Script
content:
Script encapsulates the source, hash and key count for a Lua script.
Taken from https://github.com/gomodule/redigo/blob/46992b0f02f74066bcdfd9b03e33bc03abd10dc7/redis/script.go#L24-L30

type Script struct {
	KeyCount int
	Src      string
	Hash     string
}
